order_id.description: Unique identifier for each order transaction, following the pattern ORD-XXXX.
order_id.notes: Intended primary key; a confirmed duplicate exists (ORD-1002 appears twice with identical data), which is a data quality issue. Must always be populated. Pattern constraint: ORD-[0-9]{4}.

customer_id.description: Unique identifier for the customer who placed the order, following the pattern CUST-XXX.
customer_id.notes: Contains 1 null value (ORD-1032 is the confirmed missing case). Pattern constraint: CUST-[0-9]{3}. A single customer may appear across multiple orders. Classified as PII.

customer_email.description: Email address of the customer associated with the order, used for communication and identification.
customer_email.notes: Contains 2 null values. Should conform to standard email format (local@domain.tld). Classified as PII; subject to potential GDPR compliance requirements. A single email may appear across multiple orders.

order_date.description: The date on which the order was placed, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.
order_date.notes: Must always be populated. Format: YYYY-MM-DD. Observed date range spans 2024-01-03 to 2024-02-10. order_date must not be later than ship_date; ORD-1037 is a confirmed anomaly where ship_date (2024-01-08) precedes order_date (2024-02-07).

ship_date.description: The date on which the order was shipped, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.
ship_date.notes: Contains 6 null values. Null is expected and valid when order status is 'processing', 'cancelled', or 'refunded'. Must be populated when status is 'delivered' or 'shipped'. ship_date must be greater than or equal to order_date; ORD-1037 violates this rule and is flagged as an anomaly. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

product_sku.description: Stock-keeping unit identifier for the product included in the order, following the pattern SKU-[A-Z][0-9]+.
product_sku.notes: Must always be populated. Pattern constraint: SKU-[A-Z][0-9]+. A single SKU may appear across multiple orders. Should reference a valid product in the product catalog.

product_name.description: Human-readable name of the product purchased in the order.
product_name.notes: Must always be populated. Should be consistent with the associated product_sku; mismatches between product_name and product_sku may indicate a data integrity issue.

category.description: Product category grouping to which the ordered item belongs.
category.notes: Must always be populated. Observed values are limited to {Electronics, Accessories, Furniture}; new categories may be added as the business grows. Should be validated against an allowed-values list.

quantity.description: Number of units of the product included in the order.
quantity.notes: Must always be populated. Observed range: -1 to 10. Negative values (e.g., -1 for ORD-1012) are valid only for refunded orders and must not appear for any other status. Zero quantity should be considered invalid.

unit_price.description: Price per single unit of the product before discounts or taxes are applied, in USD.
unit_price.notes: Must always be populated. Observed range: $9.99 to $749.00. Must be a positive value. Used in conjunction with quantity, discount_pct, and tax_rate to derive total_amount.

discount_pct.description: Percentage discount applied to the order, expressed as a whole number (e.g., 10 represents 10%).
discount_pct.notes: Must always be populated. Observed values: {0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25}. Must be between 0 and 100 inclusive. A value of 0 indicates no discount was applied.

tax_rate.description: Tax rate applied to the order, expressed as a decimal proportion (e.g., 0.08 represents 8%).
tax_rate.notes: Must always be populated. Observed values: {0.08, 0.25}. The standard expected rate appears to be 0.08; a rate of 0.25 on ORD-1013 is a confirmed anomaly and should not be tolerated. Must be a positive value between 0 and 1.

total_amount.description: Final monetary value of the order in USD after applying quantity, discounts, and taxes.
total_amount.notes: Must always be populated. Observed range: -$86.39 to $808.92. Negative values are valid only for refunded orders. Should be derivable as: (quantity * unit_price) * (1 - discount_pct/100) * (1 + tax_rate); deviations may indicate calculation errors. Critical column for financial reporting.

payment_method.description: The method used by the customer to pay for the order.
payment_method.notes: Must always be populated. Observed values: {credit_card, debit_card, paypal}. New payment methods may be added over time. Classified as sensitive data; potential PCI-DSS compliance applicability.

status.description: Current fulfillment status of the order in its lifecycle.
status.notes: Must always be populated. Observed values: {processing, shipped, delivered, cancelled, refunded}. Drives conditional rules: ship_date must be null for processing/cancelled/refunded; ship_date must be populated for shipped/delivered; quantity may be negative only for refunded orders.

region.description: Geographic sales region within the US associated with the order.
region.notes: Contains 1 null value (confirmed on ORD-1008). Observed values: {US-EAST, US-WEST, US-SOUTH, US-CENTRAL}. Should be populated for all non-cancelled orders. New regions may be added as the business expands.

warehouse_id.description: Identifier for the warehouse responsible for fulfilling the order, following the pattern WH-XX.
warehouse_id.notes: Must always be populated. Observed values: {WH-01, WH-02, WH-03, WH-04, WH-99}. WH-99 on ORD-1037 is a confirmed anomaly and does not correspond to a known warehouse; should be validated against a reference list of valid warehouse IDs.

is_flagged.description: Indicator of whether the order has been flagged for review due to a detected anomaly or data quality issue.
is_flagged.notes: Must always be populated. True indicates the order has been flagged; confirmed flagged orders include ORD-1012, ORD-1013, and ORD-1037. Flagging logic is incomplete: known issues such as the duplicate ORD-1002 and missing customer emails are not captured by this flag and should not be solely relied upon for governance.
